Effect: e.g. single button inputs

Name: Standard attack
Effect: A clan-specific light attack that can be repeated into a combo

Name: Heavy Attack
Effect:
After a charge-up, unleash an attack that knocks backs enemies and deals more damage .
Can break enemy blocks

Name: Feed - Enemy
Input: Near an enemy that is unaware, stunned or low health - hit the feed button
Effect:
Against enemies: A lengthy animation that sees Phyre bite the enemy, restoring blood pips, and then kills them normally with a neck-snap or similar.
Note: You are vulnerable during this.

Name: Feed - Civilian
Input: Near a Civilian - hit the feed button
Effect: Against civilians - an even longer animation - especially if the target has full resonance (e.g. they glow fully) - target is left dazed after feeding - masquerade violation (obviously)

Name: Assassinate
Input:
Near an enemy that is unaware, stunned or low health - hit the Interact button 
(e.g. E)
Effect:
A faster animation that will kill an enemy outright - no health or blood pips from this though.
Note: You are still vulnerable during this.

Name: Telekinetic Pull
Effect:
Pulls an enemy towards you allowing follow-up attacks or pull them off ledges.
Larger enemies can resist and smaller enemies will resist after the first attempt.
Also lets you grab items from around the world, including dropped weapons.

Name: Dash
Effect:
A quick burst of speed in the input direction, can be combined with attack inputs to do "dash kicks"
By default you'll hop backwards but with directional inputs you'll go towards that direction.
e.g. multiple buttons or combinations of them, or timed against enemy attacks

Name: Standard attack chain
Effect:
Repeatedly attacking will result in a clan-specific combo of light attacks - these are easily dodged and blocked by enemies.
These vary a fair bit from faster (Toreador and Banu) to more impactful and purposeful (Brujah and Lasombra) to the most unique of having additional Range (Tremere).

Name: Shunt
Effect: A very fast elbow attack that can cross distances and disrupt enemies, best used as a closer when you don't want the enemy sent flying.

Name: Parry
Effect:
Will block the triggering attack and stagger them, opening them to other attacks and counters (including disarming - see "ADVANCED COMBOS"
Notably can send enemies upwards if they were doing their own version of a jump attack.

Name: Back Kick
Effect:
A powerful push-kick that sends enemies flying, often off their feet.
Can break enemy blocks

Name: Front Knee
Effect: A strong and fast-gap closing knee.

Name: Side-Kick
Effect: A roundhouse kick that knocks all enemies within range in the input direction, staggering them - good for crowds.

Name: Slide
Effect: Slides Phyre along the ground, lowering their profile.

Name: Slide kick
Effect: Performed by attacking while sliding/on their back - equivalent to a Back Kick, but can't break guards.

Name: Drop Kick
Input:
Airborne + Crouch + Attack
(easiest input is Dash+Crouch+Attack)
Effect: A two-footed attack that does heavy damage and knockback on enemies, will "bounce" Phyre if it hits letting you land on your feet or bounce around the environment - and if it misses you'll be on your back to perform a slide-kick.

Name: TK Fire
Input: Standard attack while holding a loaded gun with TK.
Effect:
Fires the gun in quick succession, emptying its current ammo reserves (clip/cylinder/magazine/drum).
Some weapons have more shots than others.

Name: TK Throw
Input: Standard attack while holding any other item with TK.
Effect: Throws any held item, melee weapon, dismembered body part of firearm at a target, either for distraction or to deal direct damage.
DAMAGE:
Object dependent, but roughly 
low (bottle) 
medium (bat) 
lethal (knife)

Name: Drop Assassination
Input: When above an unaware (or stunned/low health) enemy - hit the interact button
Effect: Same as a standard assassination but it allows you to perform it from above while in middair - useful to quickly eliminate some enemies by jumping overtop (which is silent and faster than crawling)
Combining inputs

Name: Shunt-Disarm
Input: Shunting an enemy that has either been recently Parried or is currently Reloading their weapon.
Effect:
You can Shunt weapons out of enemies hands, so far there's two ways I know to do this.
1. During a reload - this will make the enemy drop their gun.
2. After blocking a melee attack

Name: Dash-Drop Kick
Input:
Dash + Crouch + Attack
(fash-foward and backwards work best)
Effect: Instantly activates the drop kick allowing it to be used as a gap closer or powerful attack against enemies in combat.

Name: "Death from Above"
Effect: Stomp enemies like an Italian Plumber, by jumping, dashing overhead and then performing a drop-assassination before they can notice you're there.

Name: Kick+Knee+1,2,3
Input:
Back Kick + Front Knee + Standard attack chain
(can be triggered through other kicks - e.g. slide kick, and can replace the front knee with a jump - but this is the most reliable way to trigger)
Effect:
Knocks a light enemy into the air, closes the distance with a front knee, and then starts middair juggling them with your standard attack combo - you should "stick" to the enemy in middair if done correctly, travelling with them.
Note: Different clans do this slightly differently/easily - Tremere will do it at range while still on the ground and Banu and Toreador will stick to the enemy very effectively, but Ventrue need a little more timing to pull off properly.

Name: Heavy Mixup
Effect: At any point during a standard attack chain you can hold one attack to turn it into a heavy attack, cutting down on the initial charge-up.
